title: Account Deletion (GDPR-Compliant Full Account Erasure)
description: Users can initiate permanent deletion of their account and all associated data. GDPR Article 17 compliance mandatory. Deletion is a multi-phase pipeline: soft-delete (grace period) → hard-delete (purge). Users have 30-day grace period to cancel. After hard-delete: all PII, messages (in personal/DM contexts), and media purged. Group messages deleted or anonymized per policy.

phase_1_initiation.auth: Full re-authentication required (password + 2FA if enabled). JWT alone insufficient — prevents CSRF-based deletion.
phase_1_initiation.idempotency: Only one pending deletion request per user_id. Second request returns existing request details.
server_actions: INSERT account_deletion_requests (user_id, requested_at, scheduled_hard_delete_at = now() + 30 days, status = 'pending')
Revoke all active sessions immediately (logout all devices)
Disable all bot tokens associated with account
Send confirmation email with cancel link (signed token, valid 30 days)

phase_2_grace_period.duration: 30 days (configurable per jurisdiction: Russia LGPD-equivalent may require different period)
phase_2_grace_period.cancellation: POST /v1/account/deletion-request/cancel — requires signed cancel token from email. Updates status = 'cancelled'. Account restored.
phase_2_grace_period.visibility: During grace period: account marked as 'deactivation pending'. DMs not accepted. Profile not searchable. Existing messages still visible to recipients.

phase_3_hard_delete.execution: Async background job (not synchronous API call — may take minutes to hours for large accounts)

deleted_immediately: users row (or anonymized: name → '[Deleted User]', phone/email NULLed, avatar removed)
anonymized_not_deleted: Group messages: sender_id NULLed, display_name replaced with '[Deleted User]'. Content preserved for group history integrity.
Payment records: user_id replaced with pseudonym for financial audit trail (legal requirement).
retained_for_legal_hold: Trust & Safety action records (CSAM reports, etc.) — 7-year retention per applicable law.

object_storage_purge: All signed URLs for user's objects revoked immediately.
Object keys scheduled for deletion via storage lifecycle policy (max 24h lag).
CDN cache purged for avatar and media CDN keys.

database_transaction_strategy.isolation: READ COMMITTED (no phantom risk on distinct user_id rows)
database_transaction_strategy.approach: Chunked deletes (1000 rows at a time) to avoid table lock contention. Long-running jobs use advisory locks per user_id.
database_transaction_strategy.deadlock_prevention: Fixed deletion order: messages → media_references → conversation_members → user. No circular FK dependency.

gdpr_compliance.right_to_erasure: Fulfilled by hard-delete pipeline. Response within 30 days (same as grace period).
gdpr_compliance.exemptions: Legal obligation retention (financial, CSAM) documented in Privacy Policy.
gdpr_compliance.dsar_before_deletion: User can download data archive (DSAR) before initiating deletion. Link provided on deletion initiation screen.
gdpr_compliance.data_portability: Export includes: messages, media, profile data. Format: JSON + media zip. Generated on-demand, expires 48h.

security.unauthorized_deletion: Re-auth + 2FA required. Deletion request contains signed token. Cancel requires email-delivered token — no API-only cancel to prevent account takeover post-deletion.
security.timing_attack: Deletion confirmation email sent even if account doesn't exist (to prevent account enumeration via error).
security.admin_deletion: Admin-initiated deletion (e.g., CSAM enforcement) skips grace period. Documented separately in enforcement runbook.

flow: Warning screen: 'This is permanent. All your data will be deleted.'
Optional: DSAR download prompt ('Download your data before deleting')
Reason selection (optional, for product analytics: 'Found another app', 'Privacy concerns', etc.)
Success screen with grace period countdown
